content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

All five amendments identify verified defects introduced by the retry/backoff changes. Code inspection confirms: (1) four resume tests use with_transient_failure(stage, 3) against a max_attempts of 5, (2) those same tests lack start_paused=true unlike peer retry tests, (3) BackendUnavailable from the availability path maps to retryable TransportFailure while BinaryNotFound (terminal) only fires on rarer spawn/exit-code paths, (4) tokio::time::sleep(backoff) at engine.rs:4637 has no cancellation awareness creating a TOCTOU window, and (5) timeout errors route through cleanup() which deletes artifacts instead of cleanup_failed_invocation() which preserves them.

## Votes

- `fr-1-2fc8a73c`: Accept
  Verified: all four resume tests (lines 1043, 1111, 1417, 3037) use with_transient_failure(stage, 3) while retry_policy.rs:45 sets TransportFailure max_attempts to 5. With only 3 injected failures the 4th attempt succeeds within budget, breaking assert!(first_result.is_err()). The retry_exhaustion test at line 2718 already uses count=5 as the correct pattern. Bumping to 5 is the minimal correct fix.
- `fr-1-171fc893`: Accept
  Verified: all four resume tests use plain #[tokio::test] while peer retry tests (retry_exhaustion at line 2709, cancellation_halts at line 3768, cancellation_between at line 3816) already use #[tokio::test(start_paused = true)]. With exponential backoff (5s+10s+20s per retry cycle), each test would add ~35s of real wall-clock delay. Adding start_paused=true makes tokio::time::sleep advance instantly, consistent with the existing pattern in the file.
- `fr-1-5008e57c`: Accept
  Verified: resolve_binary() (line 493), ensure_binary_available() (lines 883-895), and check_availability() (lines 1121-1132) all return AppError::BackendUnavailable for missing binaries. error.rs:302 maps BackendUnavailable to FailureClass::TransportFailure (retryable, 5 attempts). BinaryNotFound (terminal per retry_policy.rs:57) is only reached via the rarer spawn-NotFound path (line 937) or exit-code-127 path (line 1383). The common case of a missing claude/codex binary burns all 5 retries before failing, defeating the fast-fail intent. The availability/build-command path must emit BinaryNotFound directly.
- `fr-1-06c34f70`: Accept
  Verified: engine.rs:4637 performs a plain tokio::time::sleep(backoff).await. The cancellation check at line 4561 gates will_retry, but cancellation arriving after that check and during the sleep (which can last up to 60s per the backoff calculation) creates a TOCTOU window where the run sits idle instead of shutting down promptly. No tokio::select! is used anywhere in engine.rs. The standard fix of racing the sleep against cancellation_token.cancelled() via tokio::select! is correct and necessary for responsive shutdown.
- `fr-1-8b4ded53`: Accept
  Verified: timeout errors from spawn_and_wait() (lines 987-1001) flow to invoke()'s catch-all at lines 1161-1163 which calls prepared.cleanup(). For Codex backends, cleanup() (lines 55-63) calls best_effort_cleanup() which deletes temp files. In contrast, cleanup_failed_invocation() (lines 66-90) preserves artifacts by moving them to runtime/failed/. A timeout (hung backend) produces less diagnostic evidence than a normal non-zero exit, which is backwards — timeout failures are arguably more worth inspecting. The timeout path should route through the preserving cleanup.
